finding: GPTQ/Hessian output-aware ternary beats nearest by +0.51 nats (PPL 115k->69k) - assignment matters - but closes only 6% of the nearest->FP gap. Combined with RT-124 (block-scale +2.4, objective/activation no help), one-shot PURE ternary is fundamentally too lossy regardless of the PTQ toolbox; even the best stack leaves PPL in the thousands, far from FP (23) or the CE-adapted model (114). Training remains the dominant lever. Per the decision tree, a small GPTQ gain -> pure ternary codebook likely too small -> RT-127 signed-epsilon.
